review_text: The tent spots were not very nice. There was very little grass, which makes the site very dirty/muddy. All the tent sites looked directly onto a shed and rubbish bins, which took away from the overall experience. I was expecting a more natural setting with trees and a relaxed, outdoorsy feel, especially since the photos looked amazing. Unfortunately, it seems those images mainly reflect the campervan areas, not the tent sites. The pool was freezing cold even for a very hot day. The tent camping area could definitely be improved.
Staff were helpful and friendly. Lots of warm blankets which was nice on cold nights. Old fashioned bungalow - nothing fancy but does the job. Non-existent wifi or tv connection to Netflix etc.
Great little caravan park. Easy to get to and perfect for a place to stay with the kids

If I could give zero stars for service I would and I was only a visitor for a few hours ! There was someone taking drugs via syringe in the communal kitchen, we took photos and the repetition said he was a diabetic and it’s medical. Absolute BS and she refused to do anything about it and was rude. We had 5 kids with us!! Not to mention they put chemicals in the pool and didn’t circulate it properly and check PH level before kids got it. One of the children had a severe allergic reaction, they did not care. Rooms are tiny as.
Excellent customer service A++, and great location. Would highly recommend to all
Great location. I was only there for a quick stop over but I'd be happy to stay there longer in the future. Our dogs enjoyed it too. Definitely recommend.
Sanitäranlagen waren sehr verdreckt. Pool war aber sauber.

Rooms are tired and are in need of a freshen up window sills and blind rails are dirty. The bathroom was very outdated but usable after going outside to get there. Pool was green and on one occasion the BBQs didn't  work. It was quiet with plenty of bird life but I don't think we will return.

The park is very tired. The toilets were dirty, appeared not to have had a good clean for quite awhile. Corners were grotty, mirrors dirty even a used cotton bud on vanity that had been there from previous afternoon when we checked in. It is a tree studded park, and leaves are a given, but it just gave snakey vibes. Convenient location and lady on reception was very friendly, although office not open at 9.25 when we left, although advertised as 9am open.

Stayed here for 3 days I really liked the cabins. It’s not luxury but fully self contained cabins with bath and shower, full kitchen 2 rooms. One upstairs. Although it was a little warm up there the kids loved it. Has aircon and ceiling fan. I was very happy with my stay. It has a pool for adults and the little ones. It also has a tennis court and play ground for the kids. The only criticism I have is the kids play ground had no shaded area and was way too hot for the kids to play. Perhaps a shade sail could be considered so the kids and more comfortable playing.

The room was poorly maintained and for the price we paid for the Lorikeet Loft. In comparison to other Ingenia and other cabins this was by far the oldest least kept i have ever stayed in. To do general maintenance paint and small upgrades is a fair expectation I think. Disappointed  and the cost of around $200 per night is a decent amount for a cabin. Alot higher than places that are closer to attractions.
Lovely setting with really lovely staff.
Neat and clean with convenient location close to public transport. Kids loved the swimming pool.

This is the fourth time that my family and I plus our beloved large family dog, have stayed at Ingenia Sydney Hills - this year. Each time in a different cabin, all four cabins were maintained to the highest standards. On arrival, you will be greeted at reception by either the adorable Lily, or, the beautiful Louise. Anthea - Head of Housekeeping and Nadine, have consistently ensured our cabins have been serviced with efficiency, dedication and pride; keeping us with a plethora of white fluffy towels every few days. All of this was achieved with a continuous smile on each of their faces and an outstanding personalities to match their kind and welcoming hearts. Dave - Park Maintenance, planted the most beautiful plants outside KP10 Cabin, which boosted park morale significantly. Dave also promptly handled our ant issue (which is to expected due to park location), with ease and sincerity, which was greatly appreciated. Kylie - Park Manager, is singlehandedly the most hard-working manager I have ever had the pleasure of interacting with. Kylie's commitment to bettering the park and ensuring guest satisfaction is unparalleled and impressive. Her work ethic goes above and beyond what is expected, and she is an absolute delight to deal with and all of my family members look forward to interactions with Kylie on a daily basis. We have also stayed perviously at Ingenia - Avina (Vineyard), and by far Sydney Hills is superior in every aspect of the park. On behalf of myself and family I thank all staff for providing a welcoming, safe and happy environment.

We are only rating this stay a 2 out of 5, solely because the beds were very comfortable and the reception staff were genuinely lovely and kind.

When we first checked into our room, it appeared tidy at a glance. However, upon closer inspection, there were several cleanliness issues. The windows were dirty, with visible mould on and around the window sills. Most surfaces were covered in dust, as though they hadn’t been properly cleaned in some time. The bathroom had mould on the ceiling and around the toilet, and the shower temperature was extremely inconsistent—constantly switching between hot and ice cold with no stable temperature.

In the dining area, the sliding door was dirty with debris built up along the bottom, and there was an old, unpleasant smell. When we opened the cupboard to get the broom, there was a very strong and disturbing odour, as though something had died inside.

I raised these concerns with reception while we went out for a short time. The receptionist was very kind and helpful and notified the cleaning staff. However, when we returned, all the windows had been shut (I had intentionally left them open), and the mould was still present. I tested it with a baby wipe, and it came off very easily, indicating it had not been properly cleaned.

When I returned to reception to explain this, the cleaner was present. After showing them the dirty wipe and explaining that the mould had not been removed, the cleaner responded rudely and arrogantly, stating she had sprayed everything with bleach and shut the windows because dust was blowing in. I explained that dust does not cause mould and that I had taken photos before we left, which showed no change. She said she would come to the room so I could show her, which I did, but her attitude remained rude throughout the interaction.

We were also told to avoid the pool as its always having maitence done but never fixed (was green when we stay)

The room also had a lot of damage around, paint peeling from rail, slippery patio when it rained, paint peeling off wall, etc

We were extremely disappointed by both the cleanliness and the response, especially considering the price we paid and the fact that we had two young children staying in the room. The situation left us feeling upset, frustrated, and seriously considering leaving early.

As FT travelers we have stayed at many van parks across NSW, SA and Victoria. On the whole we find that parks located within cities are never very clean or amenable. We also find that the big van park chains are not that accommodating as there is always another customer rolling in. In saying that, however, our tight unattractive slot, and the atrociously dirty and unkempt amenities were on another level. I feel sorry for the families who come here seeking a “holiday”.

dame an der anmeldung war ziemlich lost.
der platz an sich war okey. hygiene könnte verbessert werden
Went for a visit as I know some one staying there not staying in a cabin

We had booked a powered ensuite site for two nights. It had not been cleaned very well. There were leaves strewn about near the entrance to the bathroom (which we swept on our own). The ensuite itself was showing its age with worn out fixtures, crumbling beams, and spider webs and dead insects stuck to the ceiling (a little cleaning with detergent could have prevented this).  The waste water drain was completely covered with leaf debris.  The grass next to our site had not been mowed for quite some time and was now full of weeds.  The access roads inside the park are quite narrow -- important when you're driving a 26ft motorhome. Unlike other Ingenia locations, park amenities for visitors are scarce -- one camp kitchen and a non-heated swimming pool.
We have really enjoyed our stays at other Ingenia RV parks such as Kingscliff and Bonny Hills.  Sadly, Sydney Hills was a huge disappointment. We will recommend our friends and family to avoid this location.

For context, we live on the road and have done for some time. We got a major ant infestation in our caravan on night 2 at this place. Never seen anything like it. But ok, it happens. My only issue was with how it was handled. They called maintenance and the guy who showed up honestly behaved like he was on something. He was super rude and aggressive for no reason, accusing me of refusing to move sites (not remotely accurate), told my husband, "well, you're in the bush, what do you expect?" (Ummm, this is the closest we've ever been to a city and never had ants like this) and then came back with some spray, did half the job and ran off like he had somewhere better to be. We ended up going to bunnings to buy our own spray and re-did the site, because the ants were flowing out of new mounds with every spray. We sorted it out, but the handling was very poor and unnecessarily unpleasant. We are not complainers, we have never left a park for almost any reason, but this situation was not reasonable and should have been dealt with professionally. It's not our fault your park has ants. I would not come back after the way this was handled.
It really needs a good clean everywhere. Probably understaffed because some areas were ok. Lots of rubbish on the ground. Nice little park though.

perfect for the price, the cabin we had was a little old, with the bathroom being a bit outdated. else all was just as expected. for us it fulfilled our needs for a base for an event around the corner.

Marre, the receptionist, is one of the most loving women I have spoken to today. She is one of the greatest people you will ever meet. She works her arse off every day to ensure everyone has a fantastic holiday and stay. She is so nice, so lovely, and very helpful to the community in the caravan park. All the other staff and receptionists who work there are also very kind. Sometimes, Marre even gives out free ice cream to the people there, and I’m definitely not one of them 🤭. So, if you are...thinking about coming to Ingeina holiday park Sydney hills trust me you won’t regret it at all it's an amazing place to be😊
It served our purpose, rlcabin was small but had everything we needed my daughters room whith bunkers was a bit dusty
Kind staff was very helpful. Great park, well maintained with lots of extras.

We really enjoyed our stay. The rooms were very clean and comfortable. The bed was just fantastic 😊. Toiletries towels were great. Nice size fridge. Good stove. Great little outdoor setting though a little tight. Had a backdoor too. The shower was lovely and hot, though a somewhat weak flow from the showerhead. But thank you for making our stay comfortable.😊👌

Room are very clean.
COOKIE ARE TO DIE FOR !!
very nice people/place some have bathroom attached to house others don’t haven’t have a bad experience.
Very close to school poc
The roof was very low so I hit my head there was no hot water and the power went out and scared my kid so we moved to a better cabin in conclusion the hotel has  good and bad things

Just overnight van site to break up the journey home - just 15 mins off the M7 (Baulkham Hills exit).  Site was compact but accessible, not sure how bigger vans (18+) would go here though. Amenities block was clean, there's a decent size pool and a tidy BBQ area etc.
Didn't do a lot of local exploring but large tavern next door looked good, fast food and takeaways also nearby, shops only a short drive away. I wouldn't say accessible to Sydney city but handy to the Hills district.
We got a different cabin than what we booked online but Kylie, the Park manager got back to us and solved the issue! Happy with the customer service!
Staying there frequently and complained again and again re the terrible WIFI speed. It's basically useless. No one cares.
Awesome place to visit. Pool is great, and an exceptional tennis court.

시드니내 조용하고 (단 주말밤엔 옆 대형레스토랑쪽에서 요란한 음악소리가 밤 11시이후에도들림)  웹사이트에서 모닥불이 가능하다하여 예약하고 왔다. 화덕관력한 온라인 문의에는 답변을 받지 못했고 리셉션에 화덕을 문의하니 카라반 파크 전지역 오픈 화이어 안된다고 안내를 받아 매우 당황스럽고 실망스러웠다. 이곳에 예약한 주요이유가 모닥불가능하다는것 이었는데 이부분이 안되니 방문할 이유가 없다.

화장실 샤워장 깨끗하고 관리 잘돼서 볓점은 4개

웹사이트에서 모닥불이 가능하다는 잘못된정보 우리같은 피해자 재발방지를 위해 조속히 수정하기바람

모닥불 피우려는 캠퍼들은 이곳에 오지마세요.

Caravan sites are tiny and tight access. Grounds and amenities are clean and tidy. Incredible stench of sewage plus trucks carrying it 7 days a week right through the park.  It's the only dog friendly place close to the city centre.
Booking my caravan site they asked for size,upon arrival the site was inadequate for my caravan and off street parking for my vehicle,very dissatisfied and with the price we pay

Der Campingplatz ist insgesamt okay – ruhig gelegen und unser Stellplatz für den Camper war gut gewählt. Was uns weniger gefallen hat: Die Geräte der Gemeinschaftsküche waren nicht sehr sauber, besonders die Heißluftfritteuse war stark verschmutzt und sogar staubig. Auch die Toiletten könnten etwas näher an den Stellplätzen liegen – die Wege waren verhältnismässig weit. Für den im Vergleich eher hohen Übernachtungspreis hätten wir insgesamt etwas mehr Komfort und Sauberkeit erwartet. Da ist auf jeden Fall noch Luft nach oben.

This is the second time I've stayed here and both times were not without issue. When I checked in I was given a different cabin to the one I booked (1 bedroom instead of a 2 bedroom). The receptionist tried to tell me the cabins were all the same and interchangeable. I showed her the pictures on the website and explained that this was not what I booked. She said that they didn't have any of the cabins I booked available. When I complained about being downgraded, she argued it wasn't a downgrade. Again, I asked for what I had booked and paid for. She said she could only offer a different cabin, but explained it wasn't clean yet. We agreed as it was bigger and thought we'd be more comfortable in it than the other one they put us in which was very small. We were wrong. The 4 of us were effectively all in the same room, albeit further apart. We all slept terribly and couldn't wait to leave the next day. If we had been given the cabin we booked in the first place, we would have been a lot more comfortable. After this experience, I will never stay there again.
It was a lovely stay.  Very clean and tidy.  Would highly recommend.
Caravan stop. No food on premises but a big hotel next door and Woolies or Aldi or Cole's just up the road. Well maintained grounds and a very nice pool and amenities.... And dog friendly.

This campground has absolutely NOTHING to do with a wonderful vacation! We booked the campsite and all you get is a gras site next to a dumping station. A lot of trucks coming every day to dump there waste right next to you. It smells disgusting. Right next to it is the kitchen. It’s a disgusting mess!! A lot of French backpackers living and working at this campground and they are acting like they own the whole kitchen! You have absolutely no space to cook, they don’t clean anything, we had no space for our food in the fridge, they stole some of our food and they are so loud during the day and night.  You can’t sit on the veranda because of the bad smell of the trucks and bins and inside is everything dirty and no space. Every day they mowing for hours. We are really disappointed. The women at the reception was friendly but not helpful. The only thing what’s good is the pool and the WiFi but you have to connect every morning again. If you want to drive with the bus then I wish you good luck to cross the street. Sometimes we waited 10 min to get to the other side, there are to many cars. No busses are driving on Sundays. We are never coming back.
Not enough room to set up for a tent and car with awning...let alone solar if you want to!

Great experience
Amazing place for kids
Staff are really friendly and great service
Great spot. Staff were friendly. Good facilities. Perfect place for a stop over.
Thanks to Kylie and her fantastic team, for a wonderful stay. Staff are friendly and were happy to accomodate our needs. Thank you!

We arrived after hours and the hotel emailed us instructions to access a lock box with a key and directions to our room. This was so helpful and appreciated.
The rooms were cute and quaint and was perfect for our family of 5.

I recently enjoyed a stay at this establishment and found it to be exceptionally clean and comfortable.  The location offered convenient access to a wide variety of activities and dining options, making it ideal for both business and leisure travelers.  I was impressed by the plentiful amenities and the overall attention to detail. I would highly recommend this accommodation to anyone seeking a pleasant and convenient place to stay.  The staff were friendly and accommodating, further enhancing my positive experience.  I look forward to returning in the future.
It's set in a good area with park like atmosphere. Set on a main road but cannot here the road noise. Clean and tidy. Used as a base for blue mountains and city, only 1 hr away.

This place is really good. Great place to relax after a long day. I stay here when working interstate. Its nice to be able to drive up and park next to your cabin with all your things. Very conveniently situated for my customers and the staff are lovely.

PROS
-Nice and cosy        -The map walkways are
named after monopoly
places
-Full Kitchen
-Pretty Clean
-Dog Friendly
-Basic
-Value for money.

CONS
-Had ants in the bunk beds.
-Fee required for Dog. ($50)
-location is a bit far.

Tent area not good at all. No shade or trees, dirty site, very little grass, terrible smell of urine and rubbish. The tent area is opposite to the rubbish bin storage area and with the wind and heat the smell was horrible. The communal kitchen area didn't look very clean. Customer service good.

Our stay at **Ingenia Holidays Sydney Hills** was fantastic! The modern cabins were spotless and cozy, surrounded by lush greenery. The kids loved the pool and playground, while we enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere and nearby trails. The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful. A perfect family getaway – we can’t wait to return!

Great place to stay,  very clean rooms, the bed was comfortable, but need a hand rail at the front door of the Kookaburra suites as I felt unstable (I'm in my 80s).
I will stay there again.
Really disgusting my kids couldn't wait to go to the pool and the water is green and dirty and they didn't take much to stay 4 days

Wasn't the best experience. Pictures on the website was nothing like it was there. Room wasn't ready when we arrived. Got put in another room which wasn't  cleaned either. Got a refund for 1 night which was good.
Facilities are very clean, with great air conditioning for hot summer days. Great value for money.

Venue is nice. Pool good, but we had to clean it first ourselves. Nice bbq area. Cabin however was not clean at all. Especially under the beds and sofa. Would expect much more considering the price. Also kitchen equipment was not enough for four people

I didn’t have high expectations, but unfortunately, my experience at Ingenia Holidays fell short of even basic common sense. When booking, I specifically asked for our cabins to be connected in terms of check-out and check-in times since we had to move cabins. But on the day of our first check out we were told we had to check out at 10 am and that they would “prioritise” our next check-in. However, we ended up waiting until the normal check-in time, just like everyone else.

To make matters worse, the second cabin had a leaking and/or broken air conditioner, which made the stay uncomfortable. Basic coordination and maintenance should be standard, and I expected better service, especially since I went out of my way to make arrangements in advance.

We needed one nights accomodation as we had a function at Vicar's next door. Located in the countryside of Sydney Hills district and approx. 30km from the CBD.|Reception was friendly and helpful.|We had a self-contained cabin (C75) Currawong Cottage. It was well appointed with a stove, microwave and fridge. There is also complementary tea & coffee unfortunately no milk. We also had a private deck and car park.|Reception does sell a few grocery items if required.|There is also a communal pool, BBQ, tennis court and playground all of which we did not use.|With a range of cabins, caravans and camping sites available.|Suited our needs for one night.

We had a great stay at the park.  Great cabin which met all our needs and accommodated our pet with a small exercise yard.  Staff were great and the location brilliant for our needs.  Definitely stay at the park again.

Read the negative reviews before we left and were glad we ignored them.  Some people expect more than their prepared to pay for... Our gain, their loss.

Unfriendly and unprofessional reception staff. After checking out I got asked to leave asap from the visitor parking when we are setting up negation! I gave my spot to a waiting car and stopped at the side for less than 3 mins and got  told "can't park there if you are not staying here" which is super unprofessional.
The cabin is ok but expect lots of noise from construction and maintenance next door. But the service wouldn't get any worse 0 stars if there is an option.
Very average
Clean and well kept. Friendly helpful staff. Perfect location for us and there’s a bus stop for the city express right outside
Wonderful people running this park. So helpful. Even before I asked a question they had me sorted. Wonderful time was had by me.

From the very beginning from booking and from the first night  Managment and staff have been fantastic, my family and I were in need of accommodation and they made sure we were ok. The facilities are clean and comfortable very happy

We stayed on a powered site for a week. Yes, sites were tight but all reviews told us that. Road is busy but very handy for everything we needed.
My issue was the amenities. Yes, they were tired but that would have been fine if they were clean. The owners can’t control the mess that park users leave but the cleaning was not happening. Toilet paper was cheap and hard to access(kept tearing and getting stuck in the holder). On the first day a “mess” was left next to the sanitary bin and it stayed there the whole week. Cleaners had smeared it a bit but made no attempt to clean it away. If something that obvious is missed, what else is not being cleaned ?
Staff were friendly but they are being let down by the service providers

Absolutely disappointed with this place. The rooms were dirty and very dated. Our family booked 3 separate rooms and one of them we had to make the beds up. I didn't want to give this rate but had to give a star. We won't be back!
A lovely welcoming place to stay with our little dogs 🥰🥰Great also that they accept cash payments 😊
Great people Great service Great park high recommend
reception staff was friendly. quiet. all amenities were kept clean. affordable price, next to restaurants for some quick snack.
Very nice place I will be using them again in the future

Staff were excellent, had a major car accident on way to NSW, car was a write off didnt make 1st night booked accommodation, when we arrived the staff were brilliant, very caring, considerate and even refunded 1st night back. Great facilities, family friendly and pet friendly we had our puppy with us, we were treated like gold. I would highly highly highly recommend anyone heading to NSW for a stay with family with pets or just on ur own, book here, close to transport n shops and hounestly just decent hard working staff that just make you feel at home. I rate this place and it's staff at 5 stars a million times over

The park is on a very busy section of road. You don’t hear any traffic noise which is good but it’s madness each time you want to enter onto the road to go anywhere. But I guess that’s just everywhere in Sydney. Site we had was good once we were in but it was a tricky site to back into and get out of
Awesome
Great place tucked away in the big smoke

Stayed in a Pet Friendly cabin for a couple of weeks. Although clean and tidy, the cabin was tired looking, door paintwork scratched, and particularly the bathroom needed a lot of attention. Looked like an attempt had been started to refresh it, but stopped part way through.
As far as amenities of the park itself, did not use them.

Great weekend  away,we will b back
Dog Friendly  Cabin
Great location. Sites are a bit small.

We have stayed at this park on a few occasions as it is close to family.
We chose an ensuite site this time.
It was a bit tricky to get onto the site, which was quite small & narrow.
The ensuite was clean, bitterly cold & dated. Paint was peeling off the ceiling & surfaces were very grotty i.e. the grout around the basin.
Some towel rails & hooks would be a great addition as there is nowhere to put things.
It is situated on a very busy area & roadways.
Having said that, no road noise is heard.
위치가 멀지 않아서 너무 좋았던곳 전체적 시설도 깨끗하고 좋았음
Customer service was top notch.
absolutely fabulous place to camp. heaps of amenities and excellent location
The reception was welcoming and the followup quick
Convenient location, close to everything, clean tidy rooms, comfy beds, good hot water pressure. Parking was ok, spots could be bigger. Otherwise great. Friendly reception staff and easy check in
Location was great, on  very very busy main road and very tight for backing in large vans
We have stayed at this caravan park multiple times and have always had a good experience. Since a recent change of management the park is looking really great and staff feel more approachable

Great little park and friendly helpful staff to assist,
Bus stop right out the front that takes you straight to the train station
Cabin not ready or clean on arrival. Ants in kitchen and bathroom. Floors dirty. Mattress not comfy. Would not recommend or maybe I won’t stay there again. Needs upgrading desperately
Perfect for a stopover in Sydney ✨️
Great location, facilities and service wish I could have stayed longer but glad my family did and loved it

Stayed rv park. It has much to offer.
Bit tight parking caravan.
Toilets and laundry works well.
Camp kitchen very small.
Very messy.
Lack of cleaning in these areas

Since big corps have taken over caravan parks everything has plummeted. This park was pretty gross. Smelt like damp cigarettes. Cars were speeding through all hours, so much maintenance needed, it’s falling apart. Lots of seedy annuals. Camp kitchen small but nice amenities very used and there is no way the cleaners could keep up with the traffic. People showering with their dog in the bathroom. Luckily it was just a quick few days stop. Receptionist was very welcoming.

I had last minute change of holiday plans and stayed here. Always planned to stay here but not at this time. Stayed in no. 13 cabin. Arrived at 2 pm my cabin was spotless and smelt beautiful! The cabins have been booked out every night I was told and can believe that cos I had to wait 2 days of my holiday to get one. But I have to say the cleaners did a fantastic job in getting mine ready in time. Check in was easy, lady on the desk on the Monday afternoon was lovely and so was the lady when I checked out on Saturday. Anyone who whinges about the cabins are being a bit unreasonable! The presentation of my cabin was faultless! Everything worked, it was well equipped and comfortable. If something was missed like a ‘full rubbish bin’ how hard is it just to empty it yourself! Yes it’s part of their job to empty it before new occupants but seriously! Go and speak at the office if you have a problem.
I give my stay a
10/10. Well done to all the staff and thanks for making my stay so enjoyable. I will be back. Hopefully with my little caravan next time.

Stayed in the powered camp sites. Facilities were clean, camp kitchen was cramped... 6 people on one campsite x 10 camp sites = 60 people trying to use 2 ovens and one fridge.

It's basically a backpackers hostel on the powered camp sites..... But it is post Covid so, amusez-vous.

The staff are fantastic, very friendly and really good at their jobs.

Rented a cabin for the weekend with a late arrival. When we got to the cabin the heater was broken, the garbage can was still full of trash. The evening was cold and we had nothing to do but wait till next morning to talk to service desk.

The lady came and investigated and yes the heater was actually not working. They were able to change us into another cabin and yes the heater in new cabin was working however the cabin was infested with ants 🐜 crawling all over the walls, floors and countertops and the bedroom windows doesn’t close. There a panty liner sticker stuck to the wall etc. The cabin was semi-clean at best.
Friendly and helpful staff, very clean facilities

Compared to the other Ingenia park in Sydney at Nepean River it is chalk and cheese. This park was nearly half the price, extremely clean compared with disgustingly dirty, and a lot quieter with a hotel next door for meals. After our experience with ingenia at Nepean rivers I was not going to use this brand of park again. Thought we would give ingenia one more try and Dural was a very pleasant surprise.

Most caravan sites are very tight for larger vans. Amenities are cleaned daily, but a bit more TLC or attention to detail is required, and avoid if possible parking a van and tow vehicle under trees, staff are friendly and do try to please, a great pub right next door has absolutely great food.

Needed accommodation at Dural and tried all day to contact Ingenia Hills Sydney.|Lots of phone calls. Left lots of messages. |They said they had call back system but no-one ever got back to me.|Frustrating and stressful when you need accommodation urgently.|Traveller from South Coast
They painted it,was really surprised,nice and clean,also looked like new washbasins in bathroom,really impressed! Thank you Ingenia,I will certainly come back again,friendly personnel as well.

Staff was great
Pool was great
Space was good
cabin room needs some deep clean / update & would give 5/5

We had booked for 2 nights in a tent. We got site 1, right next to the barbecues and kitchen. The lack of respect from the group of French people who live here is unbearable. It is clearly written that after 10pm, no noise is tolerated. Yet on the 2 nights we couldn't sleep because of the noise, and the light from the kitchen, or their scooter/car far too late at night. We had to get up very early to visit Sydney, so I was hoping at least to be able to sleep by 10pm at night. It was as much shouting as laughter. Go back to your site or close the kitchen door and stay inside. I didn't feel at ease, as if I couldn't use the kitchen because they were there all the time. Anyway I'm glad I got every last detail of their lives. Pitiful.

What's more, there are big lorries in a row with their engines running right in front of the tent sites. I understand that every space counts, but this area, opposite the maintenance shed and the rubble and dustbins, seems really neglected. But above all to maximise space. It's a shame.

The facilities were very clean on the day we arrived and the pool was pleasant in the heat.
We had a great stay, the facilities are awesome and our cabin was perfect. Lily in the front office is so kind and made our stay extra special :)

We will not be back. Booked and paid 1 month in advance for an ensuite site. The ensuites are very average. These sites are NOT suitable for large vans. Here is a hint, just because the site measures 8.5 mtrs does not mean it fits, there is a car in front towing it, usually a big one.These are motor home sites. After moving the industrial bins and manoeuvring for half hour we got in. Not a staff member in sight to assist. The slab though is under the van, not the awning, mud is under the awning. We put our floor down under the awning and when it rained, all the mud from up the hill covered our floor. 6.30am we receive a text reminding us we have to get out that day, thanks for the wake up alarm. Second and last time we stay here.

Cabin is superficially cleaned. Exhaust fan in bathroom is a health hazard. Cabin is rundown and in need of updating and deep cleaning. Flyscreens ripped, floors grubby, walls grubby, cobwebs present, Check in was easy. Road noise is loud and unrelenting till after 11.30 (on a weeknight). You can still hear it but less frequently. Basically, it's just tired and dated. Thought the warnings about not leaving the place clean were downright cheeky considering what you get at the start.
The only complaint I have is that the cabin we stayed in had a very loose aerial for the tv so made it impossible to watch.

Great site for anyone visiting Sydney. Facilities are good and clean, staff are helpful and friendly. Site is a short drive to the new metro train system. If you park at Cherrybrook station there is free parking!
One word of caution for anyone towing a caravan - Most roads in the area are pretty hilly.
Cheap and clean facilities and sites are ok

Very disappointing. Site was not big enough to put our chairs out. Very untidy and dirty.  Was a total waste of money and was way overcharged for what we got.  Booked for 2 nights but only stayed 1. Felt very unsafe as well.  Zero rating.

Loved our stay and found the park and the staff exceptional. I would highly recommend a stay here to relax and unwind in a family friendly quiet environment. Staff were exceptionally friendly and helpful. Will definitely plan to stay here again with the opportunity to further explore the area.
Wanted somewhere close to Kenthurst, cheap, clean and comfortable. Stayed at the Dural caravan park 40 years ago for 6months and loved it then. Long time between visits but still a great little spot.

When we first got to the Holiday Park we were driving around for ages because the map is extremely inaccurate. When we finally found the cabin there was no parking. And the place looked NOTHING like the pictures. It was tiny, its completely false advertisement (which by the way is suable). Also the couch is disgusting there are stains everywhere

I am currently on an 8 day stay, and was pleasantly surprised at my first impression.  Upon arrival I was greeted with a friendly receptionist who was so helpful.  The room was clean and all needs met for my small family.
The grounds are well kept and I would like to extend my thanks to the hard working staff that work tirelessly to ensure a clean and friendly holiday park
Very clean and the staffs are friends

They only thing this place has going for it is the friendly staff and a clean pool / BBQ area.
When we entered our kookaburra cabin the only bed that was made was the double. The double bed is on casters and rolled around everytime you got on it. The blankets are really old. Nothing felt clean inside, my first thought was omg what am I going to catch.
Skirting was not water proof so there were bits of wood chips around the room after our 2 day stay. Which worried me they would charge me a cleaning fee. Thank goodness they didn't.
If you use the dishes and utensils you're relying on the cleanliness of others because you are required to clean them or incurred and cleaning fee.
Our cooker in our room did not cook well, took 2 hours to cook a steak and boiled potatoes. Had to use the BBQ the second night which did work.
The games room had a broken table tennis table held together with clamps and if you bumped it, it would come lose. We used the paddles and one broke as we were playing.
The soap they provided was not the best for sensitive skin, my daughter got a burning sensation.
Unfortunately it was not the best place I have been to and will never return. My advice is take your own cooking stove, soap, utensils and bedding.
Proof of stay can be provided.

Have not stayed over.

Looking neat from the road
Beautiful littel caravan park. Big pool. Lots of nice people  dog friendly 😊 closes to most thinks

Hands down the grossest and scabbiest family holiday park we’ve ever been in. The room was old and decaying (literally), explicit graffiti all over my children’s bedding, a cabin view of debris and an eyesore of a caravan. The experience was awful. No bedroom door to master. Avoid. You’re better off literally anywhere else. It wasn’t even cheap. Zero stars.
Great service. Very well maintained
Nice and clean. Very nice staff.

Staff are super friendly! BUT..
We were there for a weekend and booked a powered site tent.
Sites are too small, very close to each other (if you like a bit of privacy then it is an issue) so that I could here the other couple in the beside tent wispering! power sites are not in shade at any time of day. Not much of open field infront of tent(if you have a kid )

At the first day of our stay there was a huge rubbish bin infront of our site which was disgussting. After that there was a leaking in the water system again infron of the tent sites and for the whole second day we had a noisy plumming truck infront of the tent doing plumming stuff.
Ok for a night
Was a cheap place to stay close to shops

Booked this caravan park for two separate one night stays on our journey through to Queensland and back to Melbourne. One of the only caravan parks who are dog friendly. They provided fantastic service, we cancelled our stay 12 minutes prior to check in as a courtesy to let them know we were now unable to make it anymore (it was school holidays and I know they were booked out) and did not expect a refund however we didn’t even have to ask and they processed it! Thank you guys so much!
Excellent cabin/caravan park in a great location. LOVED the visit from a kookaburra.

What a hidden gem this park is! We have a 30' caravan and were allocated a great site, near the Immaculate amenities. The staff are extremely helpful and obliging.  Dural is very central to everything and by using the motorways makes for easy navigation to all areas in Sydney.
We will be back. Thankyou!
Really nice place
Great family oriented cabin

Ladies at reception are always so helpful. Nothing is too hard for them.

Great sites for a busy place. Lots of trees and open spaces to charge solar if needed. Ask for sites 2-4 if need more sun.

After providing credit card payment over the phone on 2 seperate occasions after the booking money was taken from my account for over $300  Never got a clear answer why it happened. Would check to make sure it hasnt happened to you also
Van blocks not level Makes it hard for the elderly

Stayed at Igenia recently.  Durell site was amazing.  Ann was the lady I spoke to over the phone - she worked at another location but facilitated my stay.  travelling with a large dog is sometimes very difficult but she was awesome.  Site location/cleanliness/affordability was all on point.  Highly recommend.
Nice and quiet. Peaceful. Needs quite a bit of gardening work.
Well equipped, clean , easy to book and check-in.
We had overnite stop over with pets. Staff Kerry was very helpful. Very quiet, clean, well equipped cabin.
Good place to stay. Near lots of things. Going back this weekend.

Although the bathroom is clean and the shower door is very clean and no water leaking to the floor the rooms are very small and the cabin overall is small. Also it is kinda hard to park our car in the cabin.
Convenient budget accommodation but Room was very dusty, bugs in beds, fluffy / furry blankets full of dust and loose thread caused allergic reactions.
Self catering, budget friendly, accessible accommodation. Nothing more, nothing less. Love it.

Wonderful holiday park nestled in Dural. Wonderful clean cabins and plenty of Caravan space. Customer service fantastic. Close to shops, tourist attractions and we loved the flexible book in. Kitchen was easy to cook in and all pots and pans provided to cook up a storm.extra hot shower with plenty of body wash and hair care. Bed was very comfortable including electric blanket. Love to stay again soon..
Very clean and well played out park
The blankets were dirty. I don't think they were ever washed. Plenty of dark long hair. Yuck. The lady behind the front desk was lovely though.

Stayed here in April 2023 just before Easter long weekend and we loved it there. Sites were a bit tight just took a little bit more manoeuvring than normal but once in it was good. Was a quiet Park which was great and  staff were helpful and welcoming. Bus stop right in front which is really handy. We loved our stay there and will definately stay again.
